name: DevOps Tools
description: Client-side generators and validators for infrastructure, CI/CD and networking — configs, manifests and scripts you can copy straight into your project.

tools.name: IP Subnet Calculator
tools.description: Calculate network, broadcast, host range and mask from a CIDR block, with a visual bit breakdown.

tools.name: curl Command Builder
tools.description: Build a curl command from a form and export the equivalent Python requests and JS fetch code.

tools.name: Nginx Config Generator
tools.description: Generate Nginx configs for reverse proxy, SSL termination, static sites and load balancing.

tools.name: Kubernetes YAML Generator
tools.description: Generate a linked Deployment, Service and Ingress in one manifest from a simple form.

tools.name: GitHub Actions Generator
tools.description: Generate a CI workflow YAML from ready-made Node, Python and Docker templates.

tools.name: robots.txt Generator
tools.description: Build a valid robots.txt with allow/disallow rules, crawl-delay and sitemap, with live validation.

tools.name: .env Validator
tools.description: Validate .env syntax and flag likely leaked secrets and API keys before you commit them.

tools.name: Crontab Builder
tools.description: Build a cron expression and validate it against a real parser, with the next 5 run times previewed.

tools.name: Backup Script Generator
tools.description: Generate a cron-ready database backup script for Postgres, MySQL or MongoDB, with an optional S3 upload.

tools.name: Health Check Config Generator
tools.description: Generate a ready-to-paste health-check script and JSON config for uptime monitors from an endpoint.

tools.name: Subnet Overlap Visualizer
tools.description: Paste multiple CIDR blocks or IP ranges and see their coverage and overlaps on a visual map.

tools.name: IP Address Info & Classifier
tools.description: Classify an IP as private, public, CGNAT, loopback or reserved with plain-language context and reverse-DNS.

tools.name: Systemd Timer Explainer
tools.description: Explain and build systemd OnCalendar expressions in plain English, with the next 5 run times.
